auth_type: Basic Auth or Static API Key
auth_notes: Most legacy dispatch systems utilize Basic Authentication (Base64 encoded credentials) or a static key passed in a custom header (e.g., 'X-API-KEY'). Some may require a session-based login where a token is retrieved via a POST request and used in subsequent calls.
rate_limits: Typically unmanaged or strictly limited to 1-2 requests per second; often lacks standard rate-limit headers.
gotchas: Returns data in XML format requiring manual parsing via the XML node or Code node
Uses non-standard HTTP status codes (e.g., returning 200 OK for internal server errors)
Requires fixed IP whitelisting or VPN access if the system is hosted on-premise
client_must_provide: Sample XML/JSON request and response payloads
Firewall access/IP whitelisting for n8n cloud/server
research_notes: Integration must be built using the HTTP Request node. If the system is SOAP-based, the developer will need to construct XML envelopes manually in the body. Response data will likely need a 'Code' node to normalize field names into a format usable by downstream modern SaaS nodes.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration must be built using the HTTP Request node. If the system is SOAP-based, the developer will need to construct XML envelopes manually in the body. Response data will likely need a 'Code' node to normalize field names into a format usable by downstream modern SaaS nodes.
